free_text: Neutron Cross Sections of Samarium-147 and Samarium-150
RPI Linear Accelerator
Transmission measurement
Time-of-flight with a flight path of 25m.
6Li glass scintillator 0.5 in. thick.
Area analysis
Shape analysis for two resonances below 20 eV
in 147Sm.
Corrections applied for the presence of 149Sm for the
thermal transmission data. This amounted to 5% for
150Sm and 20% for 147Sm.
No information on source of uncertainties.
Approved by author.
Data taken from main reference.
Data added
BIB update. Converted to REACTION
formalism. Subentries 4 - 7 removed.
BIB correction
Converted to new date formats, lower case.
0.70 g. of samarium sesquioxide enriched to 99.48% in
147Sm, mixed with aluminum powder, and pressed into
compacts. Thin sample 0.127x10**3 atoms/b; thick
sample 0.963x10**3 atoms/b.
Other isotopic impurities not observable except in the
case of a tiny dip due to the 8.0 eV 152Sm resonance.
Resonance energy and capture width added
to data. Data for bound resonance updated.
0.38 g. of samarium sesquioxide enriched to 99.97% in
150Sm, mixed with aluminum powder, and pressed into
compacts. Thin sample 0.03055x10**3 atoms/b; thick
sample 0.4872x10**3 atoms/b.
Other isotopic impurities not observable.
